name: Abigail Adams Apple Pan Dowdy
recipeInstructions:
Blend until mealy. Sprinkle a little ice water over dough, enough to hold together. Roll out to 1/4 inch thickness, brush with 1/4 cup melted butter. Cut pastry in half. Place halves on top of each other. Roll and cut again. Repeat until you have 16 separate pieces piled up. Chill for 1 hour. Roll pastry again and cut in half. Line bottom of baking dish. Save the other half for the top.
FILLING:
Peel and slice the apples. Mix with sugar and spices and put in pastry-lined dish. Combine molasses with butter and water. Pour over apples. Cover with top crust and seal. Bake at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 10 minutes. Then reduce heat to 325 degrees Fahrenheit. "Dowdy" the dish by cutting the crust into pieces with a sharp knife. Bake for one hour. Serve hot with ice cream or whipped cream.
